pi_items.source: Code analysis: counted 5 @Test methods in OrderManagerTest (testExactlyTenItemsQualifyForBulkDiscount, testElevenItemsAlsoGetDiscount, testSubtotalDoesNotOverflowForLargeOrder, testInvalidPaymentCardIsRejected, testValidPaymentCardIsAccepted)

Analysis of buggy code: Bug 1 (> 10 threshold) causes testExactlyTenItemsQualifyForBulkDiscount to fail. Bug 2 (int overflow) causes testSubtotalDoesNotOverflowForLargeOrder to fail. Bug 3 (always return true) causes testInvalidPaymentCardIsRejected to fail. testElevenItemsAlsoGetDiscount passes (11 > 10), testValidPaymentCardIsAccepted passes (no validation logic).

pi_items.verified_value: Current code uses: if (totalItems > 10) on line 56 of OrderManager.java. Javadoc at line 44 documents: 'BUG: The bulk discount threshold is > 10 instead of >= 10'

pi_items.metadata_value: Change int total to long total in calculateSubtotalCents
pi_items.verified_value: Current code uses: int total = 0 on line 31 of OrderManager.java. Javadoc at line 22 documents: 'BUG: The accumulator is declared as int. For large orders, the intermediate sum can exceed Integer.MAX_VALUE (~$21 million), silently wrapping to a negative value.'

pi_items.metadata_value: Implement actual validation in validatePaymentCard() instead of always returning true
pi_items.verified_value: Current code returns true unconditionally at line 88 of OrderManager.java. Javadoc at line 77 documents: 'BUG: This method always returns true, performing no actual validation. Invalid cards — including empty strings, short numbers, and non-numeric values — are silently accepted, allowing fraudulent transactions to proceed.'

pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 19-25 and README.md scoring table: 25+30+25+10+5+5=100
verifier.py line 196: 'passed = score >= 70 and all_tests_pass' where all_tests_pass is defined as 'tests_run >= expected_tests and tests_failed == 0 and tests_error == 0'
pom.xml lines 22-27: dependency on junit 4.12 with test scope

privileged_info_summary: The ecommerce-service project is a synthetic Maven-based Java application (Java 11, JUnit 4.12) with 5 test methods in com.ecommerce.OrderManagerTest. The project contains three intentional bugs in OrderManager.java documented in Javadoc: (1) discount threshold uses > 10 instead of >= 10 on line 56, causing 10-item orders to miss bulk discount; (2) subtotal accumulator uses int instead of long on line 31, causing overflow for orders exceeding ~$21M; (3) validatePaymentCard() always returns true without validation on line 88. The test suite is designed to catch these bugs: testExactlyTenItemsQualifyForBulkDiscount fails due to bug 1, testSubtotalDoesNotOverflowForLargeOrder fails due to bug 2, testInvalidPaymentCardIsRejected fails due to bug 3. Initially 3 of 5 tests fail. Scoring: Bug 1 fix (25 pts), Bug 2 fix (30 pts), Bug 3 fix (25 pts), all tests pass (10 pts), test file unmodified (5 pts), VLM bonus (5 pts), total 100 points. Pass threshold: ≥70 points AND all tests pass with 0 failures.
